name: Trellis
long_description:
The next hospitality platform will actually operate listings

We believe short-term rentals are going through a platform shift: the winning platform will not just help guests find homes, but operate those homes autonomously with agents.

Our first goal is to prove agents can automate short-term rental operations at scale by partnering with property managers and replacing their fragmented software stack with Trellis agents.

We’ve had the fastest ever growth in this industry

In two months, we’ve grown to $500k ARR across 35 customers and 3,000 listings, growing around 15% week over week. One customer is already running with no human involved in day-to-day operations.

**we've previously built agents and lived the operations**

Lodo, CEO, studied mathematical logic at Cambridge, then joined Conduit, YC W24, as a founding AI engineer building customer service agents. I also scaled a property management to 150 properties and $2M in annual revenue.
Jan, COO, spent the last three years in short-term rental tech as a founding GTM, building relationships with hundreds of property managers

Listings should be operated by agents

Property management is exactly the kind of work agents are good at: coordinating communication and real-world work across guests, owners, cleaners, maintenance, and vendors. Once an agent has the full operational context, it can run the process end-to-end.

We’re building software agents can operate and reshape

We are not adding AI to static SaaS. Hospitality operations are too dynamic for fixed software. Trellis is AI-native software: the fixed infrastructure is the trellis, and the workflows, interfaces, and business logic agents grow for each operator are the vines.

Airbnb’s moat is crumbling

Airbnb won the last platform shift by solving discovery and trust: it made homes easy to find and made people comfortable booking from strangers. But in the agent era, discovery becomes less defensible because booking agents can search the open web. What remains is trust.

Today, Airbnb creates trust by validating human service through reviews. But human service is fallible, and Airbnb cannot directly control execution. Once agents run the service layer, trust can be guaranteed by the software controlling the operation.

Partnering with property managers is the fastest path to agent-run listings

We start with property managers because they already run hospitality at scale. By becoming the system that runs them, Trellis can create the first network of agent-run homes and eventually become the network guests and booking agents trust directly.
one_liner: The operating layer for the next hospitality platform
